nodes.label: Carbon and its Compounds
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text:
## Syllabus outline
- Covalent bonding in Carbon
- Catenation and tetravalency
- Homologous series and Functional groups
- Saponification & Soap cleaning action

Pedagogical concepts and learning benchmarks for Class 10 active curriculum.

nodes.label: Soaps do not work in hard water because they form scum (insoluble precipitate) w
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: Soaps do not work in hard water because they form scum (insoluble precipitate) with Calcium and Magnesium ions. Detergents solve this!

nodes.label: Carbon can neither lose 4 electrons nor gain 4 electrons due to energy constrain
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: Carbon can neither lose 4 electrons nor gain 4 electrons due to energy constraints. It only shares!

nodes.label: Catenation is carbons superpower. No other element can form such incredibly long
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: Catenation is carbons superpower. No other element can form such incredibly long stable chains.

nodes.label: Successive members of a homologous series differ by a -CH2- group which translat
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: Successive members of a homologous series differ by a -CH2- group which translates to 14 unified atomic mass units.

nodes.label: Soap has a bipolar nature: a hydrophilic head that loves water, and a hydrophobi
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: Soap has a bipolar nature: a hydrophilic head that loves water, and a hydrophobic tail that grabs the grease molecule.

nodes.label: Today we study Carbon and its Compounds. Carbon has atomic number 6, meaning it
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: Today we study Carbon and its Compounds. Carbon has atomic number 6, meaning it has 4 valence electrons. To achieve stability, it shares electrons, forming covalent bonds.

nodes.label: Ethanol vs ethanoic acid functional groups
nodes.subject: science
nodes.text: How to distinguish -OH and -COOH in one line? Esterification test links both — smell of ester in lab. Ethanol has alcohol group; ethanoic acid has carboxylic — turns blue litmus red.

For Saponification, keep the sodium hydroxide concentration exact, otherwise soap fails to set. Can anyone provide a clean explanation or an intuitive shortcut? Don't stress, I had the same doubt. In our school's mock test, the teacher explained that Amoeba pseudopodia ingestion is holozoic nutrition. The food vacuole moves inside cellular fluid. This totally simplifies the algebraic/chemic
